[[Marley Leuluai]] (great nephew) }} }} '''Phillip Leuluai''' (born 16 July 1977) is a former [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} or occasional {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]]. ==Background== Leuluai in [[Greenlane]], [[Auckland]], [[New Zealand]]. He is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] heritage. ==Playing career== An [[Otahuhu Leopards]] junior, Leuluai previously played for the [[Eastern Tornadoes]], [[Newtown Jets]] and [[Cronulla Sharks]]. He joined [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] on 27 August 2007 after their Aussie head coach [[Shaun McRae]] signed him on a {{frac|2|1|2}}-year contract for an undisclosed figure. He joined the French club [[Lézignan Sangliers]] for the 2010/11 season.{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/salford/8810877.stm|publisher=BBC News|title=Saints' Smith signs Salford deal|date=12 July 2010}} ==Representative career== Leuluai represented [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]] in the 1997 [[1997 New Zealand rugby league season|Super League Challenge Cup]], and Auckland South in the [[1999 New Zealand rugby league season|1999 National Provincial Competition]].{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=3727|title=Rugby League: Injury gives Hoppe a starting chance|author=Jessup, Peter|date=16 March 1999|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|accessdate=3 November 2011}} He toured Australia with the [[New Zealand Residents]] in 2000.[http://www.encyclopedia.com/doc/1P1-33276866.html Johns named halfback and captain] ''AAP Sports News (Australia)'', 10 October 2000[http://www.encyclopedia.com/doc/1P2-17491463.html NZ Residents end trip with a flourish] ''The Press'', 27 July 2000 In 2002 Leuluai toured France and the United States with [[New Zealand Residents rugby league team|New Zealand 'A']].[http://www.nzrl.co.nz/page/20021.html 2002 NZ A] {{Webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20080718175759/http://www.nzrl.co.nz/page/20021.html |date=18 July 2008 }} ''NZRL Official Site'' Leuluai was named in the [[Samoa national rugby league team#2008 World Cup squad|Samoa training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] but did not make the final squad.{{cite web|url=http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750|title=Samoa name World Cup Squad|publisher=League Unlimited|date=5 August 2008|accessdate=2008-08-06|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110717060051/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750|archivedate=17 July 2011}} In 2009 he was named as part of the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] side for the [[2009 Pacific Cup|Pacific Cup]].[http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/7999.php Poching names strong Samoan squad] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091010154654/http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/7999.php |date=10 October 2009 }} ''therhinos.co.uk'', 6 October 2009 ==References== {{reflist}} == External
